messages.content:
You are an expert Spanish linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in Spanish with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

en_unspecified_2324 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener ni pies ni cabeza | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to have neither feet nor head; used literally to describe an object or depiction lacking those parts.

en_unspecified_2324 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener ni pies ni cabeza | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be incoherent, illogical, or make no sense; to lack internal consistency.

en_unspecified_2325 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener ni zorra idea | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to not have even a fox's idea; used literally only in humorous or jocular contexts.

en_unspecified_2325 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener ni zorra idea | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to have no clue at all; to be completely ignorant or unaware about something.

en_unspecified_2326 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener pelos en la lengua | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to have no hairs on the tongue; anatomically unlikely and used mainly as an image.

en_unspecified_2326 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener pelos en la lengua | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to speak plainly and directly; to be frank, outspoken, or blunt without holding back.

en_unspecified_2327 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener remedio | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to have no remedy or cure; used literally of illnesses, ailments, or problems that cannot be medically or practically fixed.

en_unspecified_2327 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener remedio | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be hopeless or beyond help; to be irreparable or incapable of being corrected.

en_unspecified_2328 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener un pelo de tonto | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: to not have a single hair of foolishness; a literal reading describing the absence of a specified hair.

en_unspecified_2328 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener un pelo de tonto | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be far from foolish; to be shrewd, astute, or completely sensible and not at all naive.

en_unspecified_2329 | sense 1 | idiom: no tener vuelta de hoja | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally: not to have the turning of a page; there is no additional page to turn or review.

en_unspecified_2329 | sense 2 | idiom: no tener vuelta de hoja | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: to be final, decisive, or beyond dispute; not subject to change or further discussion.

en_unspecified_2330 | sense 1 | idiom: no va más | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally: it does not go further; it no longer moves or proceeds.

en_unspecified_2330 | sense 2 | idiom: no va más | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Casino/gambling usage: announcement used to signal that betting is closed and no further bets will be accepted.

en_unspecified_2330 | sense 3 | idiom: no va más | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: something is finished or cannot continue; that is the end of the matter.

en_unspecified_2330 | sense 4 | idiom: no va más | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: the ultimate or unsurpassable example of something; the best or most extreme instance.

en_unspecified_2331 | sense 1 | idiom: no van por ahí los tiros | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally: the shots are not going in that direction; the projectiles are not aimed that way.

en_unspecified_2331 | sense 2 | idiom: no van por ahí los tiros | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: that is not the point or approach; you are mistaken about the line of reasoning or the intended direction of events.

en_unspecified_2332 | sense 1 | idiom: no vendas la piel del oso antes de cazarlo | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally: do not sell the bear's skin before hunting the bear.

en_unspecified_2332 | sense 2 | idiom: no vendas la piel del oso antes de cazarlo | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: do not assume you will obtain or profit from something before it is secured; do not count on success prematurely.

en_unspecified_2333 | sense 1 | idiom: no vender una escoba | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally: not to sell a broom.

en_unspecified_2333 | sense 2 | idiom: no vender una escoba | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figuratively: to be unable to sell anything or to have a product or person that attracts no buyers; to perform very poorly in sales or lack commercial appeal.

en_unspecified_2334 | sense 1 | idiom: no venir a cuento | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to be mentioned or to arise in the course of a conversation or situation.

en_unspecified_2334 | sense 2 | idiom: no venir a cuento | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be irrelevant, inappropriate, or unrelated to the matter under discussion; to be beside the point.

en_unspecified_2335 | sense 1 | idiom: no venir al caso | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to pertain to the case or matter being considered.

en_unspecified_2335 | sense 2 | idiom: no venir al caso | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be irrelevant or not pertinent to the topic at hand.

en_unspecified_2336 | sense 1 | idiom: no ver la hora de | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to be able to see the time (expressing a focus on the expected moment).

en_unspecified_2336 | sense 2 | idiom: no ver la hora de | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to be very eager or impatient for something to happen; to be unable to wait for a particular event.

en_unspecified_2337 | sense 1 | idiom: no ver ni pies ni cabeza a | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: not to see either the feet or the head of someone or something, i.e., to see no discernible parts or structure.

en_unspecified_2337 | sense 2 | idiom: no ver ni pies ni cabeza a | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to find something incoherent, illogical, or without sense; to perceive no order or meaning.
